name: Quick Chocolate Pecan Fudge
recipeIngredient: 5 bars (1.5 ounces each) Dark Chocolate, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Line an 8-inch square baking pan with aluminum foil, leaving a 2-inch overhang on two opposite sides.
2. Lightly butter the bottom of the foil-lined pan.
3. Place chocolate and butter in a large heatproof bowl.
4. In a medium heavy saucepan, combine sugar and heavy cream. Cook over medium-low heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until the sugar is dissolved. Use a pastry brush dipped in water to remove any sugar crystals clinging to the side of the saucepan.
5. Continue cooking without stirring until the mixture comes to a full boil. Boil for 5 minutes, then pour the hot syrup over the chocolate mixture. Let stand for 30 seconds, then stir until smooth.
6. Stir in marshmallow crème and vanilla extract. Pour the mixture into the prepared pan and arrange pecan halves on top.
7. Refrigerate for 1 hour or until set.
8. Using two foil handles, carefully lift the fudge out of the pan and invert it onto a cutting board. Turn the fudge over and cut into 25 pieces.
9. To toast pecans, spread them on a baking sheet and bake at 350°F for 8 to 10 minutes or until toasted.
Yield: 25 pieces
Difficulty: Intermediate
Preparation: 30 minutes plus cooking and setting times
